description: A hole leak vulnerability exists in V8's Ignition bytecode compiler due to an incorrect hole-check elimination in `VisitDoWhileStatement` (src/interpreter/bytecode-generator.cc). The compiler uses a bitmap-based `HoleCheckElisionScope` mechanism to track which `let`/`const` variables have already been hole-checked, eliding redundant checks as an optimization. In a do-while loop with a non-trivially-boolean condition, the compiler correctly wraps the loop condition in a `HoleCheckElisionScope` when `loop_builder.break_labels()` is non-empty (i.e., when there are `break` statements targeting the do-while loop itself). However, it fails to account for labeled break statements that target an *outer* scope (e.g., `break target` where `target` is a label on an enclosing switch statement). Such outer labeled breaks are handled by the outer `ControlScopeForBreakable`, not by the do-while's `ControlScopeForIteration`, so they do not appear in `loop_builder.break_labels()`. As a result, when the loop body contains only outer labeled breaks (not local breaks), `loop_builder.break_labels()->empty()` returns true, and the loop condition is visited *without* a `HoleCheckElisionScope`. Any hole-check state updates from evaluating the condition (e.g., `x=1` in `(x=1) && false`) are incorrectly propagated to the outer scope. At runtime, when the outer labeled break fires, the loop condition is never actually reached, so the variable remains uninitialized (`the_hole`). But since the hole check was statically elided based on the incorrectly propagated state, accessing the variable after the labeled break does not raise an exception, leaking `the_hole` into JavaScript.

fixes.evidence_text: b371b4f8ba073fb5c054273e8909bee5de574b35 [interpreter] Merge hole elision info on break
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/7026071 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
